title: IaC Policy Violations
dataSources: OPA/Sentinel policy check results, CI/CD pipeline logs
spl:
index=iac sourcetype="policy_check"
| where result="DENY"
| stats count by policy_name, workspace, resource_type
| sort -count
description: Policy-as-code (OPA/Sentinel) prevents non-compliant infrastructure from being provisioned. Tracking blocked deployments validates governance.
value: Policy-as-code (OPA/Sentinel) prevents non-compliant infrastructure from being provisioned. Tracking blocked deployments validates governance.
grandmaExplanation: Policy-as-code (OPA/Sentinel) prevents non-compliant infrastructure from being provisioned ‚Äî so code changes that fail, leak secrets, or skip checks are caught early.
implementation: Ingest policy check results from CI/CD pipelines. Track denied provisions by policy and team. Alert on repeated violations (may indicate training need). Report on policy effectiveness and most-violated rules.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Policy engine output (CI/CD integration).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: OPA/Sentinel policy check results, CI/CD pipeline logs.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest policy check results from CI/CD pipelines. Track denied provisions by policy and team. Alert on repeated violations (may indicate training need). Report on policy effectiveness and most-violated rules.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="policy_check"
| where result="DENY"
| stats count by policy_name, workspace, resource_type
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**IaC Policy Violations** ‚Äî Policy-as-code (OPA/Sentinel) prevents non-compliant infrastructure from being provisioned. Tracking blocked deployments validates governance.

Documented **Data sources**: OPA/Sentinel policy check results, CI/CD pipeline logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Policy engine output (CI/CD integration). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iac; **sourcetype**: policy_check. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=iac, sourcetype="policy_check". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where result="DENY"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by policy_name, workspace, resource_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (violations by policy), Table (denied provisions), Line chart (violation trend), Pie chart (by resource type).
visualization: Bar chart (violations by policy), Table (denied provisions), Line chart (violation trend), Pie chart (by resource type).
